response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile and experienced engineer with a strong track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and full-stack web development, but the role demands deep Java/Kotlin expertise, JVM internals knowledge, and hands-on experience with enterprise frameworks like Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate — none of which are meaningfully present in this resume. His six-month stint at National Instruments writing C/C++ components for a C IDE is the closest relevant experience, but it is brief and not JVM-based. There is no mention of Kotlin anywhere, and Java appears only in passing contexts. This candidate is not a fit for a JVM-focused enterprise IDE role.
strengths: Genuine background in developer tooling and IDE-adjacent work (NI LabWindows/CVI, MCP code generators, CI/CD infrastructure)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, trunk-based development, pairing, and clean architecture across multiple roles
Broad systems experience spanning embedded, mobile, desktop, and web — demonstrates adaptability and depth of engineering thinking
concerns: No Kotlin experience whatsoever; not listed in skills or any role description
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework experience anywhere in the resume
Primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang — JVM is peripheral and not demonstrated at depth
